records.ety: [L. Laconicus Laconian, Gr. <?/<?/, fr. <?/<?/ a Laconian, Lacedæmonian, or Spartan: cf. F. laconique.]
usage: -- Laconic, Concise. Concise means without irrelevant or superfluous matter; it is the opposite of diffuse. Laconic means concise with the additional quality of pithiness, sometimes of brusqueness.

defs: Expressing much in few words, after the manner of the Laconians or Spartans; brief and pithy; concise; brusque; epigrammatic. In this sense laconic is the usual form.
quotes: I grow laconic even beyond laconicism; for sometimes I return only yes, or no, to questionary or petitionary epistles of half a yard long.
His sense was strong and his style laconic.

defs: Laconian; characteristic of, or like, the Spartans; hence, stern or severe; cruel; unflinching.
quotes: His head had now felt the razor, his back the rod; all that laconical discipline pleased him well.
